rulings.Case Title: YOVANNA PECK VS. CLARENCE A. THOMPSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MARCH 20, 2008 LINE 6. PLAINTIFF YOVANNA PECK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF DUTY AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS AN INVOLUNTARY BAILEE AND/OR HAD THE RIGHT TO POSSESS THE DOG. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON CLAIM OF GROSS NEGLIGENCE CLAIMS BY FAILING TO PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE OF DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350(B) AND AS A CONSEQUENCE, MAY NOT CHALLENGE DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK WILLIAMS VS. VIA LEE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike; Courtesy Copy Of Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, MARCH 20, 2008 LINE 13. DEFENDANTS PAUL WARTELLE, FRANCIS PINNOCK AND WARTELLE, WEAVER & SCHREIBER'S, Special Motion To Strike IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTED FACTS ESTABLISHING A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. ONCE MS. LOW'S MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT BY THE COURT WAS DENIED, THERE WAS NO BASIS FOR DEFENDANTS TO CONTINUE TO PROSECUTE THE WRONGFUL EVICTION CASE. BY SEEKING TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT, LOW WAS AFFIRMING THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT TO SETTLE. THE FACT THAT SHE WAS FOUND TO BE IN BREACH DID NOT GIVE HER THE RIGHT TO DECLINE TO DISMISS THE WRONGFUL EVICTION WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF WAS MAKING A CLAIM TO RECOVER ALL OR PART OF WHAT HE HAD PAID IN THE SETTLEMENT, HE WAS NOT SEEKING TO RESCIND IT AND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM WAS NOT A BASIS TO CONTINUE THE WRONGFUL EVICTION ACTION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE PARTIES' ARE TO PROVIDE CASE LAW SUPPORTING THEIR CONTENTIONS THAT GOING UP THE BACK STAIRS AND PHOTOGRAPHING PLAINTIFF WHILE SERVING HIM CONSTITUTES AN INVASION OF PRIVACY. =(302/PJM/AY)
